skill.name: signup-flow-cro
skill.description: Provides conversion rate optimization guidance for signup, registration, account creation, and trial activation flows.
skill.summary: Optimize signup flows by reducing friction, improving form UX, and planning conversion experiments.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged weak cryptography and system reconnaissance patterns, but review found only plain-language CRO guidance in SKILL.md. No executable code, command use, data exfiltration, or prompt-injection attempt was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Terms
low_findings.description: The flagged lines contain marketing and UX words such as description, desktop, redesign, design, and field labels. They do not describe cryptographic algorithms, code, or any security-sensitive implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged content is prose inside SKILL.md and contains no cryptographic API usage or executable instructions. The matches appear to be substring collisions in ordinary CRO terminology.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Terms
low_findings.description: The flagged lines discuss paid account creation and password UX recommendations. They do not instruct the model to inspect host identity, enumerate the system, or run reconnaissance commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is non-code guidance for signup forms, with no shell commands or environment inspection. No evidence found of system reconnaissance behavior.

content.value_statement: Signup friction lowers trial starts and account creation rates. This skill helps teams audit forms, reduce unnecessary steps, and plan focused conversion tests.
actual_capabilities: Audits signup and registration flows for unnecessary fields, unclear steps, and completion friction.
Recommends field order, labels, help text, validation behavior, and error handling improvements.
Compares single-step and multi-step signup patterns for B2B, B2C, waitlist, and trial flows.
Suggests mobile signup improvements including touch targets, keyboard types, autofill, and reduced typing.
Defines measurement plans for completion rate, field drop-off, errors, time to complete, and auth method mix.
Generates A/B test ideas for layout, copy, authentication options, trust elements, and trial requirements.
limitations: It does not run analytics queries or inspect product telemetry by itself.
It cannot guarantee conversion lift without testing on real traffic.
It provides UX and CRO guidance, not legal advice for privacy or compliance requirements.
It needs accurate flow details to produce specific recommendations.

use_cases.title: SaaS Trial Signup Audit
use_cases.description: Review a free trial signup flow and identify fields, steps, and copy that may reduce completion.

use_cases.title: Mobile Registration Cleanup
use_cases.description: Improve mobile account creation with better input types, fewer typing tasks, and clearer validation.

use_cases.title: Experiment Backlog Planning
use_cases.description: Turn signup problems into prioritized quick wins, larger changes, and A/B test hypotheses.

prompt_templates.title: Quick Signup Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner audit for an existing flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this signup flow for conversion friction. Flow type: [free trial, freemium, paid, waitlist]. Required fields: [list fields]. Steps: [number and names]. Current completion rate: [rate if known].

prompt_templates.title: Reduce Required Fields
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate these signup fields and classify each as essential, useful, or deferrable. Explain what to keep, remove, or move to onboarding: [field list and business reason for each].

prompt_templates.title: Design a Better Signup Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Redesign this signup flow for higher completion. Audience: [B2B or B2C]. Product: [summary]. Current steps: [steps]. Constraints: [compliance, verification, billing, or sales needs]. Provide field order, microcopy, validation, and success-state recommendations.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Test Roadmap
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a prioritized signup CRO experiment roadmap. Include quick wins, high-impact changes, and A/B test hypotheses. Use these metrics: [form start rate, completion rate, field drop-off, error rate, mobile split, auth method mix].

output_examples.input: A B2B SaaS trial requires email, password, full name, company, phone, role, and team size before product access.
output: High priority: Move phone, role, and team size to onboarding unless sales qualification is required before access.
Medium priority: Keep company optional or infer it from the work email domain.
Test idea: Compare email plus password first against a two-step flow with profile details after account creation.

output_examples.input: A mobile app signup has a three-screen flow, delayed errors, small buttons, and email verification before exploration.
output: Improve mobile completion by using email keyboard type, larger touch targets, autofill, and inline validation.
Delay verification until the user reaches a gated action if risk allows it.
Add clear resend and change-email options to reduce verification dead ends.

output_examples.input: A waitlist signup asks for email, name, company, role, budget, timeline, and open-text use case.
output: Use email as the primary required field and make one segmentation question optional.
Replace long open-text input with selectable use-case options when possible.
Confirm the waitlist status immediately and explain what happens next.

best_practices: Start with the minimum data needed before product access, then collect the rest later.
Separate proven quick wins from hypotheses that need controlled testing.
Review desktop and mobile flows because friction often differs by device.
anti_patterns: Do not require sales or profile fields at signup unless they are essential before access.
Do not hide password requirements until after a failed submit.
Do not optimize only for form completion if downstream activation quality declines.

faq.question: Can this skill redesign a full signup flow?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide the current steps, fields, audience, and constraints, and it can propose a revised flow.

faq.question: Does it work for both B2B and B2C signup?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes patterns for B2B SaaS trials, B2C apps, waitlists, and e-commerce account creation.

faq.question: Can it analyze real conversion data?
faq.answer: It can interpret metrics that you provide, but it does not access analytics systems automatically.

faq.question: Will it always recommend fewer fields?
faq.answer: No. It recommends fewer upfront fields when possible, while respecting compliance, billing, and verification needs.

faq.question: Can it create A/B test ideas?
faq.answer: Yes. It can generate test hypotheses for fields, layout, authentication options, copy, trust elements, and trial terms.

faq.question: Is it a replacement for compliance review?
faq.answer: No. It can flag UX tradeoffs, but privacy, consent, and regulated workflows still need qualified review.
